description: Stoner gas system with rotating bolt

bones.name: Weapon Root
bones.description: Lower receiver - root of all weapon bones
bones.placement: Place at the base of the lower receiver, where the grip meets the frame

bones.name: Upper Receiver
bones.description: Upper receiver - static relative to lower
bones.placement: Place at the front takedown pin, where upper meets lower receiver

bones.name: Bolt Carrier
bones.description: Bolt carrier group slides rearward under gas pressure, compresses buffer spring, then returns forward to chamber next round
bones.placement: Place at the rear of the bolt carrier group, centered in the upper receiver channel

bones.name: Bolt

drivers.description: Bolt rotates 20.7 degrees as carrier travels back. Cam pin rides in carrier track: dwell for first 3mm, then linear rotation over next 5.4mm, then dwell for remaining travel

parameters.source: US Patent data, mil-spec bolt rotation

bones.description: Bolt head rotates to lock/unlock from barrel extension. 7 lugs engage at 20.7 degrees rotation. Cam pin in carrier slot forces rotation
bones.placement: Place at the bolt face, centered on the bore axis

bones.name: Trigger
bones.description: Trigger rotates ~15 degrees around the trigger pin. Releases hammer when pulled
bones.placement: Place at the trigger pin hole center in the lower receiver

bones.name: Selector
bones.description: Fire selector switch. Rotates between Safe (0), Semi (90), and Auto (180) positions. Blocks trigger movement in Safe
bones.placement: Place at the selector lever pivot on the left side of the lower receiver, above the grip

bones.name: Charging Handle
bones.description: Slides rearward 76mm to manually cycle the bolt carrier. Latched by a roll pin when forward
bones.placement: Place at the rear of the upper receiver, centered on top where the charging handle sits

bones.name: Magazine
bones.description: Detachable box magazine. Unparented when released for reload animation, re-parents to Weapon Root when inserted
bones.placement: Place at the top of the magazine, where it meets the magazine well in the lower receiver

bones.name: Magazine Release
bones.description: Button that releases magazine catch. Pressed inward ~3mm to release magazine
bones.placement: Place at the magazine release button on the right side of the lower receiver, just above the magazine well

bones.name: Dust Cover
bones.description: Ejection port dust cover. Springs open ~75 degrees when bolt cycles. Protects internals when closed
bones.placement: Place at the dust cover hinge pin on the right side of the upper receiver

bones.name: Forward Assist
bones.description: Pushes bolt carrier forward to ensure full lockup. Pawl engages serrations on bolt carrier
bones.placement: Place at the forward assist button on the right side of the upper receiver, behind the ejection port

bones.name: Bolt Catch
bones.description: Holds bolt carrier open after last round. Magazine follower pushes it up. Operator presses to release bolt
bones.placement: Place at the bolt catch pivot pin on the left side of the lower receiver

bones.name: Hammer
bones.description: Rotates ~35 degrees under spring tension to strike firing pin. Caught by disconnector during cycling
bones.placement: Place at the hammer pin hole center in the lower receiver, behind the trigger

bones.name: Buffer Spring

drivers.description: Buffer spring compresses as carrier moves back. At full travel (95mm), spring is 30% shorter

bones.description: Buffer spring in receiver extension (buffer tube). Absorbs carrier energy and returns it forward
bones.placement: Place inside the buffer tube, at the front of the spring where it contacts the buffer

bones.name: Cam Pin
bones.description: Rides in carrier cam track to force bolt rotation. Rotates with bolt, visual indicator of lock state
bones.placement: Place at the cam pin hole in the bolt, perpendicular to the bore axis

bones.name: Extractor
bones.description: Spring-loaded claw that grips cartridge rim. Pivots ~5 degrees to snap over rim during chambering
bones.placement: Place at the extractor pivot point on the bolt face, at the 3 o'clock position

physics.source: Sprinco standard buffer spring specs, mil-spec BCG weight
